ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the test */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Description of the test */,
  "test_type" INTEGER /* Type of the test (e.g., SQL operation, API call) */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the environment where the test is executed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the test was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the test was last updated */,
  "test_status" TEXT /* Current status of the test (e.g., planned, in progress, completed, failed) */,
  "test_owner" INTEGER /* User responsible for the test */,
  "test_duration" INTEGER /* Expected or actual duration of the test */,
  "test_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of test execution (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_test_type FOREIGN KEY ("test_type") REFERENCES test_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_id") REFERENCES environments ("environment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test being executed */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label of the test result */,
  "response_code" INTEGER /* Response code from the test */,
  "response_message" TEXT /* Response message from the test */,
  "thread_name" TEXT /* Name of the thread executing the test */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the response */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the test was successful */,
  "grp_threads" INTEGER /* Number of threads in the group */,
  "all_threads" INTEGER /* Total number of threads */,
  "sample_count" INTEGER /* Number of samples in the test */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Number of errors in the test */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the test was executed */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Time taken for the test to complete */,
  "test_environment_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the environment */,
  "additional_metadata" TEXT /* Additional metadata related to the test result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environments" (
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment */,
  "environment_name" TEXT /* Name of the environment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the environment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the environment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the environment was last updated */,
  "environment_status" TEXT /* Current status of the environment (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "environment_type" TEXT /* Type of environment (e.g., development, staging, production) */,
  "environment_config" TEXT /* Configuration details of the environment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("environment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the test type (e.g., SQL Operation, API Call) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test type */,
  "type_category" TEXT /* Category of the test type (e.g., functional, performance, security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the test group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test group */,
  "test_ids" TEXT /* Array of test IDs included in the group */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the group was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the group was last updated */,
  "group_status" TEXT /* Current status of the test group (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "group_owner" INTEGER /* User responsible for the test group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_groups_test_ids FOREIGN KEY ("test_ids") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA engineer, admin) */,
  "user_status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the test or result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test being accessed */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test result being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the test or result was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access (e.g., 1 hour, 1 day) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test version */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test being versioned */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the test */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the version was last updated */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Current status of the version (e.g., draft, published) */,
  "version_owner" INTEGER /* User responsible for the version */,
  "version_metadata" TEXT /* Additional metadata related to the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_versions_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_versions_version_owner FOREIGN KEY ("version_owner") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test being logged */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test result being logged */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., INFO, ERROR, DEBUG) */,
  "log_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the log entry was created */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log (e.g., test, environment, user) */,
  "log_user" INTEGER /* User who generated the log */,
  "log_environment" TEXT /* Environment where the log was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_log_user FOREIGN KEY ("log_user") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_log_environment FOREIGN KEY ("log_environment") REFERENCES environments ("environment_name")
)
